message:
refactor(pipeline): remove consecutive-role validation warning

The validate_session() function previously warned when two consecutive
messages shared the same role (e.g., two User messages in a row),
flagging it as something that "may confuse target agent." In practice,
this warning produced false positives for several legitimate reasons:

1. Codex sessions can have consecutive user messages even after
   deduplication when multi-turn user input is split across entries.
2. Claude Code sessions sometimes emit consecutive assistant messages
   when tool results are interleaved as separate response blocks.
3. Other providers (Gemini, Cursor) have their own multi-part message
   patterns that don't follow strict user-assistant alternation.

Since this warning was more noise than signal and the pipeline already
handles role sequences correctly during conversion (re-indexing and
enrichment don't depend on strict alternation), removing it reduces
false alarm fatigue without losing any actual quality signal.

The corresponding assertion in pipeline_test.rs
(validate_session_warnings_and_info_for_quality_issues) is updated to
no longer expect the "Consecutive User" warning string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
